article_title: Lufthansa Exercises Option to Take 90% of ITA Airways for 325 Million Euros, Pushing Italian Flag Carrier Fully Inside the German Group
summary: All five snapshots reachable; Spohr and Eberhart quotes verbatim; transaction structure (€325M / 49% / June 2026 / Q1 2027 close / 10% remainder option in 2028) traced. Allowlist gaps are non-reader-facing.

content_preview.title: Lufthansa Exercises Option to Take 90% of ITA Airways for 325 Million Euros, Pushing Italian Flag Carrier Fully Inside the German Group
content_preview.summary: Lufthansa will exercise its option in June to buy an additional 49% of ITA Airways from the Italian state for 325 million euros, lifting its stake to 90% and triggering full integration of the Italian flag carrier.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Lufthansa Group said on May 12 that it will exercise its option in June to acquire an additional 49% of ITA Airways from Italy's Ministry of Economy and Finance for 325 million euros, lifting its holding in the Italian flag carrier from 41% to 90%, [according to the Lufthansa Group newsroom](https://newsroom.lufthansagroup.com/en/lufthansa-exercises-option-to-acquire-a-majority-stake-in-ita-airways/). The transaction is subject to clearance by the European Commission and the U.S. De...

recommendations: Expand the source allowlist to include newsroom.lufthansagroup.com (canonical primary for Lufthansa announcements) and the aviation trade press (aerotime.aero, centreforaviation.com).

editor_notes.content_quality: Clean News piece, 624 words. Covers the transaction structure (€325M / 49% lifting Lufthansa to 90%, Italian state retains 10% / option 2028) cleanly. Integration-already-in-flight section is editorially useful — shows that operational integration has been moving for over a year before the equity step.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all five reachable snapshots. source-0 (Lufthansa Group newsroom) confirms €325M, June 2026 option exercise, January 17 2025 first tranche date, Q1 2027 expected close, EC + US DOJ regulatory gate, and Spohr verbatim: 'Following the acquisition of the first 41 percent stake in ITA Airways last year, we promised the fastest airline integration in our history', 'All customer-facing interfaces are already integrated today—with the exception of North Atlantic flights', 'Lufthansa Cargo has been marketing ITA Airways' cargo capacity, which alone corresponds to the additional capacity of three Boeing 777 freighters'. source-1 (Euronews) corroborates and adds the Italy MEF seller framing. source-2 (AeroTime) confirms the Star Alliance March 31 2026 join date, Volare-to-Miles&More frequent flyer migration, Frankfurt+Munich terminal moves, the SWISS/Austrian/Brussels integration precedent, and the Eberhart 'a clear demonstration of the good work carried out so far' quote verbatim. source-3 (CAPA) confirms the 2028 option for remaining 10%, the EC+DOJ regulator pair, and the Eberhart quote 'Full integration into the Lufthansa Group will allow us to compete more strongly in international markets, while continuing to bring to the world the value of Italy' verbatim. source-4 (Travel Tomorrow) corroborates Spohr's 'fastest airline integration' quote.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific verified: €325M, +49% lifting stake to 90%, June 2026 option exercise, Q1 2027 close, EC + US DOJ regulatory gates, Italy MEF as seller (59% pre-deal), 10% residual + 2028 option, ITA's January 17 2025 first transfer, Star Alliance March 31 2026, Volare→Miles&More, Frankfurt+Munich terminal moves, SWISS/Austrian/Brussels precedent.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of Lufthansa or ITA Airways.
concerns: Four of five sources not in allowlist (Lufthansa newsroom, AeroTime, CAPA, Travel Tomorrow). All four are canonical aviation primaries or trade press.
recommendations: Expand allowlist for aviation primaries and major aviation trade press.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE. All quotes verbatim, all transaction specifics traced, no concerns about substance or attribution.
